title: Caldo Verde

overview.description: A traditional Portuguese soup of potatoes, onions, kale and chorizo in a rich chicken stock.

method: Heat the olive oil over a medium heat.
Cook the chorizo until lightly browned and it has released some of its oil.
Remove half of the chorizo and reserve for serving.
Cook the onion in the remaining oil and chorizo fat for 8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the potatoes and chicken stock.
Bring to the boil, then simmer for 20 minutes until the potatoes are tender.
Stir in the shredded kale and cook for 5 minutes until just tender.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Serve topped with the reserved chorizo.

planner.freezer-label: freeze before adding the reserved chorizo garnish

learning.did-you-know: Caldo Verde is considered Portugal's national soup and is traditionally served at festivals and family celebrations.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to create a silky potato soup and use cured sausage to add depth of flavour with only a small amount.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A comforting soup that uses everyday ingredients already found in the Chapman Kitchen while introducing a Portuguese classic.
chapman-tips: Slice the kale as finely as possible so it softens quickly and keeps its delicate texture.
Use floury potatoes for the smoothest soup.
Your homemade chicken stock provides plenty of flavour without needing additional seasoning.
Add the kale towards the end so it stays bright green.
If you have homemade ham stock, use it in place of the chicken stock for an even more traditional flavour.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months. Add freshly crisped chorizo after reheating if desired.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently without boiling to preserve the kale's colour.
